message:
fix(ci): use brew bash for macOS installation tests

The install-test job was failing on macOS because it was running `ru --version`
with the system Bash 3.2, which doesn't meet ru's requirement of Bash >= 4.3.

Changes:
- Added "Install bash 5 (macOS)" step to install-test job
- Modified "Check version output" and "Check help output" steps to explicitly
  use brew-installed bash on macOS via `$(brew --prefix)/bin/bash`

This mirrors the approach used in the test suite job which already installs
bash 5 on macOS.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -93,6 +93,12 @@ jobs:
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
 
+      - name: Install bash 5 (macOS)
+        if: runner.os == 'macOS'
+        run: |
+          brew install bash
+          echo "$(brew --prefix)/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
+
       - name: Run installer
         run: |
           chmod +x install.sh
@@ -106,10 +112,21 @@ jobs:
           echo "✓ ru is installed and executable"
 
       - name: Check version output
-        run: /tmp/ru-test/ru --version
+        run: |
+          # Use brew bash on macOS since ru requires bash >= 4.3
+          if [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
+            "$(brew --prefix)/bin/bash" /tmp/ru-test/ru --version
+          else
+            /tmp/ru-test/ru --version
+          fi
 
       - name: Check help output
-        run: /tmp/ru-test/ru --help | head -20
+        run: |
+          if [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
+            "$(brew --prefix)/bin/bash" /tmp/ru-test/ru --help | head -20
+          else
+            /tmp/ru-test/ru --help | head -20
+          fi
 
   version-check:
     name: Version Consistency
